Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants an analysis of a provided event log for a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process The specific tasks are Identify cases with significantly longer resolution times Determine potential root causes escalations wait times delays Explain how these factors lead to increased cycle times and propose insights recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Event Log Case 101 Start 2024 03 01 08 00 End 2024 03 01 10 15 Total Time 2 hours 15 mins 135 mins Path Receive Triage Assign L1 Investigate Resolve Close Standard fast

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Start 2024 03 01 08 05 End 2024 03 02 09 15 Total Time 25 hours 10 mins 1510 mins Path Receive Triage Assign L1 Escalate L2 Investigate Resolve Close Wait times Assign to Escalate 2 5 hrs Escalate to Investigate 2 5 hrs Investigate to Resolve 19 hours overnight delay

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Start 2024 03 01 08 10 End 2024 03 01 09 30 Total Time 1 hour 20 mins 80 mins Path Receive Triage Assign L1 Investigate Resolve Close Standard very fast

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Start 2024 03 01 08 20 End 2024 03 02 08 30 Total Time 24 hours 10 mins 1450 mins Path Receive Triage Assign L1 Investigate Resolve Close Wait times Assign to Investigate 3 5 hrs Investigate to Resolve 19 hours overnight delay

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Start 2024 03 01 08 25 End 2024 03 03 09 30 Total Time 49 hours 5 mins 2945 mins Path Receive Triage Assign L1 Investigate Escalate L2 Investigate Resolve Close Wait times Investigate to Escalate 50 mins Escalate to Investigate 28 hours 50 mins massive overnight delay Investigate to Resolve 19 hours another overnight delay

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Normal Cases 101 135 mins 103 80 mins These are resolved within a single day usually under 2 5 hours Delayed Cases 102 25 1 hrs 104 24 1 hrs 105 49 1 hrs These take 1 to 2 plus days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Root Causes Escalation to Level 2 Agents Cases 102 and 105 involve escalation Case 105 has a massive 28 plus hour gap between escalation and the next investigation Case 102 has a 2 5 hour gap to escalate and a 2 5 hour gap before L2 investigates

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Overnight delays The 19 hour gap Cases 102 104 and 105 all share a very specific pattern an investigation happens late in the day around 14 00 and the resolution does not happen until the next morning around 08 00 to 09 00 This represents a 19 hour gap strongly suggesting a lack of after hours support or a strict 9 to 5 workflow where work stops at the end of the day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Initial Assignment Delays Case 104 took 1 hour 10 mins just to get assigned and another 3 5 hours to start investigation Case 102 took 55 mins to assign Compared to Case 103s 20 mins

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Develop Explanations and Recommendations Factor 1 The End of Day Bottleneck Overnight delays Explanation Tickets investigated in the afternoon are left pending until the next morning This doubles the cycle time for tickets that cross the end of day boundary

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Recommendation Implement an SLA for end of day handoffs introduce a late shift after hours support team or empower Level 1 agents with more authority to resolve common issues without waiting for next day approvals

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Factor 2 Level 2 Escalation Bottlenecks Explanation Once escalated tickets sit in a queue Case 105 waited over a day just for a Level 2 agent to look at it

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Recommendation Review Level 2 staffing levels Implement a triage rule to escalate immediately if an issue is known to require Level 2 skipping the Level 1 investigation step which happened in Case 105 wasting time Create a dedicated fast track queue for escalations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Factor 3 Inefficient Initial Triage and Assignment Explanation Case 104 sat for over 4 hours before investigation even began

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Recommendation Automate ticket routing based on keywords category to reduce manual assignment time Set strict SLAs for the Assign and First Response Investigate steps for example must be assigned within 15 mins

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Refine Check against all prompt requirements Ensure tone is professional and analytical Format clearly with headings bullet points and bold text for readability Self Correction during drafting Make sure to explicitly calculate the durations so the user sees the math behind the significantly longer claim
